questions.q: MITRE ATLAS is the AI/ML analog of ATT&CK — it covers attacks on AI and machine learning systems rather than traditional IT infrastructure.
Which type of ML API response provides the most information for mapping decision boundaries?
Passive ML reconnaissance includes examining model cards on HuggingFace and published research papers about the target model.
In the Clearview AI case, what reconnaissance method was used to build the training dataset?
options: Scraping 3+ billion public images from social media

questions.q: A model that returns very high confidence (>0.95) on most inputs, including adversarial examples, is best described as:
options: A well-calibrated model with strong robustness
An overconfident model — likely a CNN-based architecture

questions.q: A substitute (proxy) model is trained by querying the target API and using the API's responses as training labels — no access to the target's weights is needed.
What is the 'transfer rate' in a substitute model attack?
options: The speed at which API queries are sent to avoid rate limits
The percentage of adversarial examples crafted on the substitute that also fool the real target

questions.q: Returning only hard labels (no confidence scores) from an ML API is an effective defense against substitute model training.
The ART (Adversarial Robustness Toolbox) is used for:
options: Encrypting model weights to prevent extraction
Crafting and evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses in ML systems

questions.q: In the GPT-4 model extraction research, what was inferred about GPT-4's architecture using approximately $200 in API queries?
options: Its hidden dimension size by analyzing the output embedding space
Its full weight matrix by decoding API token probabilities

questions.q: ML supply chain attacks can be executed simply by uploading a convincing lookalike model to a public registry like HuggingFace.
Why is `torch.load()` dangerous when loading models from untrusted sources?
options: It permanently deletes model weights after loading
It uses Python's pickle format, which can execute arbitrary code on load

questions.q: Pinning a model by its SHA256 hash instead of just its name protects against supply chain typosquatting attacks.
In the 2024 JFrog discovery, what technique did malicious HuggingFace models use to execute code?
options: SQL injection via model metadata fields
Pickle deserialization exploit triggered on torch.load()

questions.q: The recommended PyTorch parameter to prevent pickle-based code execution when loading a model file is:
Black-box access — where an attacker can only send inputs and observe outputs through an API — is sufficient to craft adversarial examples against a target model.
The HopSkipJump attack is notable because it finds adversarial examples using only:
options: Full white-box access to the model's gradients and weights
The model's final hard label output — no confidence scores or gradients needed

questions.q: In the ChatGPT training data extraction attack, researchers prompted the model to 'repeat the word poem forever' to trigger a divergence that emitted memorized training data.
Differential privacy during training is a defense against which black-box attack capability?
options: Membership inference and training data extraction from model outputs
Decision boundary mapping via confidence score analysis

questions.q: LLM black-box attacks differ from vision model attacks primarily because:
options: LLM inputs are discrete tokens, making gradient-based methods inapplicable
LLMs cannot be queried through APIs and require local model access

questions.q: Adversarial examples exploit the geometry of neural network decision boundaries — small perturbations in specific directions can cross boundaries while remaining imperceptible to humans.
Which adversarial attack method is considered the strongest first-order attack and is the standard for evaluating adversarial robustness?
options: FGSM (Fast Gradient Sign Method) — a single-step attack
PGD (Projected Gradient Descent) — a multi-step iterated attack

questions.q: The 2017 physical adversarial stop sign experiment demonstrated that adversarial examples only work in digital pipelines and cannot survive real-world printing and lighting conditions.
In the PGD attack formula, the epsilon (ε) parameter controls:
options: The maximum allowed perturbation size (L∞ norm constraint)
The number of gradient descent steps performed

questions.q: Adversarial training — training on adversarial examples — is currently the most effective known defense against adversarial examples.
ML-based security tools like AI antivirus classifiers are immune to adversarial evasion because they use learned features rather than fixed signatures.
In the Cylance AI bypass, what technique was used to evade the ML antivirus engine?
options: Encrypting the malware payload with a novel cipher algorithm
Appending benign strings from a video game executable to shift the feature vector

questions.q: Functional evasion of an ML malware detector means the modified malware sample both evades detection AND preserves its malicious functionality.
The GAMMA framework applies what technique to automatically evade ML malware detectors?
options: Gradient descent over a differentiable malware feature space
Brute-force mutation testing across all possible byte sequences

questions.q: Which defensive approach makes ML malware detectors harder to evade by making their features unpredictable to the attacker?
options: Feature randomization — varying the features used at inference time
Increasing the training dataset size with more malware samples

questions.q: A typical production ML pipeline has only one attack surface: the model serving endpoint.
CI/CD configuration files (like GitHub Actions workflows) can reveal ML pipeline architecture including data storage locations, model registries, and deployment infrastructure.
In the Microsoft Tay attack, what pipeline vulnerability did attackers discover and exploit?
options: An unpatched SQL injection in the Tay admin portal
A 'repeat after me' feature that fed user input directly into the model's training pipeline without filtering

questions.q: If a model registry like MLflow has no authentication on the internal network, what high-impact attack becomes trivial for an attacker with internal network access?
options: Replacing the production model with a backdoored version
Increasing the model's inference latency via resource exhaustion

questions.q: An ML pipeline's retraining schedule (e.g., weekly cron) is relevant to an attacker because it determines the poisoning window — the time window during which injected data will be incorporated into the model.
Membership inference attacks exploit the fact that ML models tend to output higher confidence on their training samples than on held-out data.
Which formal defense provides mathematical privacy guarantees that limit what membership inference attacks can reveal?
options: Encrypting the model weights with AES-256 after training

questions.q: Model inversion attacks reconstruct approximate representations of training data using only black-box access to the model's outputs.
In the Facebook membership inference research, what sensitive attribute could be inferred by querying the ad targeting API?
options: Whether a user had joined a sensitive health group (e.g., HIV+ support)
Whether a user's account was created with a real or fake identity

questions.q: Reducing the precision of confidence scores returned by an ML API (e.g., rounding to 2 decimal places) has no effect on membership inference attack accuracy.
Universal adversarial perturbations (UAPs) are image-agnostic — a single perturbation can cause misclassification on most inputs simultaneously.
A backdoored model (BadNets) will typically fail standard accuracy tests because the backdoor degrades its performance on clean inputs.
What makes clean-label backdoor attacks particularly difficult to detect?
options: They require insider access to the target's training infrastructure
The poisoned training samples appear correctly labeled to human reviewers

questions.q: Which tool uses anomaly detection in the feature space to detect backdoor triggers by finding classes whose minimal trigger is unusually small?
options: Neural Cleanse — reverse-engineers triggers for each class and checks for size anomalies
Foolbox — generates adversarial examples to test model robustness

questions.q: UAPs crafted against one model architecture (e.g., ResNet) often transfer and fool other architectures (e.g., VGG, EfficientNet).
Model extraction attacks can produce a functional substitute model without ever accessing the target model's weights or architecture directly.
Gradient inversion attacks break the privacy guarantee of federated learning by:
options: Intercepting encrypted communications between federated clients and the server
Reconstructing original training data from the gradient updates clients share with the server

questions.q: The cost asymmetry in model extraction (stealing vs. training from scratch) makes it economically unviable for small to medium ML models.
In the KnockoffNets extraction of the Acme Sentiment Classifier scenario, what was the cost ratio of stealing vs. building?
options: Over 2,000x cheaper to steal ($240 API+GPU) than to build ($500,000 training)
Roughly equal cost — model extraction requires similar compute to training

questions.q: A key defense against model extraction is to return only hard labels (no confidence scores) from a public ML API.
Data poisoning attacks can target a model's availability (degrade accuracy), integrity (cause targeted misclassification), or fairness (introduce demographic bias).
In the Microsoft Tay attack, the poisoning was delivered through which unfiltered pipeline channel?
options: A compromised admin API that accepted direct model weight updates
A 'repeat after me' feature that incorporated user-provided phrases into the model's training

questions.q: Slow, gradual model corruption via a feedback loop is harder to detect than a sudden catastrophic attack because performance metrics degrade gradually.
Activation clustering detects backdoor triggers by finding:
options: Unusual spikes in GPU utilization during model inference
An anomalous small cluster of samples in the penultimate layer's activation space for a given class

questions.q: In the AcmeBot poisoning scenario, individual poison samples each appeared normal — the malicious bias was only apparent in aggregate across thousands of injected samples.
The complete ATLAS adversarial lifecycle covers 11 tactics, from reconnaissance through to impact on the ML system.
The Microsoft Tay kill chain completed from initial access to complete model shutdown in approximately:
options: 3 weeks at a cost of $50,000 in API queries

questions.q: The NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF) provides the defensive counterpart to ATLAS. Its four core functions are:
The Clearview AI operation demonstrated that ATLAS-style threats require sophisticated technical exploits and cannot be executed using only publicly available data.
Which ATLAS tactic did the Clearview AI operation primarily use when harvesting 3+ billion facial images from public social media platforms?
options: TA0008 Collection — gathering data at massive scale from public sources
TA0006 Defense Evasion — bypassing platform security controls
